title.rendered: It Takes a Lot to Laugh, It Takes a Train to Cry

content.rendered:
Firing up the waaaaaayback machine today and travelling to Newark State College on May 15, 1971, so we can catch Dr. Zoom and The Sonic Boom performing Bob Dylan’s bluesy “It Takes a Lot to Laugh, It Takes a Train to Cry.”

It’s a great recording for its age, and you can even hear Steve introduce the song, and Bruce and Garry banter after it.

Bruce’s version is decidedly fiercer than Dylan’s 1965 original:

Performed just twice by the band in May 1971–as far as we know, Bruce hasn’t played it publicly since.

It Takes a Lot to Laugh, It Takes a Train to Cry
First performed: May 14, 1971 (Asbury Park, NJ)
Last performed: May 15, 1971 (Union, NJ)

© April 23, 2018

excerpt.rendered: Performed only twice, way back in 1971: Dr. Zoom and the Sonic Boom covers Bob Dylan’s “It Takes a Lot to Laugh, It Takes a Train to Cry.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: It’s All Over Now, Baby Blue
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Stop what you're doing and listen. The Bruce Springsteen Band's cover of Bob Dylan's "It's All Over Now, Baby Blue" is one of their finest pre-E Street moments.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Train Ride
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Bruce wasn't Steel Mill's only songwriter. Co-lead vocalist Robbin Thompson contributed his own song to the band's set lists, but their version was a far cry from Mercy Flight's original.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Alexander’s Ragtime Band (Zoom Theme)
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Half a century ago and twice only (thankfully): Bruce adapted Irving Berlin's 1911 classic as the short-lived theme song for Dr. Zoom and the Sonic Boom.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Last Night in Texas
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Let's kick off summer with Dr. Zoom and the Sonic Boom in this rewritten version of Sonny Boy Williamson's "One Way Out."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me: Cry to Me
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: During the Born to Run Tour, Bruce worked up a simmering, slow burn of a cover of Solomon Burke's 1962 classic.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Roll of the Dice: Southside Shuffle
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce turns the spotlight on Southside Johnny when Dr. Zoom and the Sonic Boom play the only known performance of "Southside Shuffle."
